focus: Axis 6 personas and UX tone: SPAR personas, teammate-style specify prompts, and Key vs Optional follow-up split compared against system-spec-kit gate, command, voice, and agent prompt patterns.

graphEvents.label: SPAR Key vs Optional follow-up budget
Codex/system-spec-kit global voice and gate protocol

label: SPAR persona source currently includes six personas, not the five named in the research focus; adapt as an expandable audience map rather than runtime roleplay.
SPAR Key vs Optional follow-up split maps directly to system-spec-kit consolidated prompt UX and should become Required To Proceed vs Optional Refinements.
SPAR teammate tone should become a context-aware intake preface, not a wholesale replacement for hard gate language.
Named SPAR personas belong in design and evaluation fixtures, not inside command runtime prompts that already carry strict voice and gate contracts.
Hard gates remain authoritative even when intake copy becomes more conversational.
External evidence includes persona docs, runtime skill role instructions, and retrospective prompt recommendations.
Internal evidence centers on global voice, command first-message protocols, clarification caps, and role-specific agent contracts rather than named personas.

direction: Inject Vera/Pete/Tess/Maya/Max as named runtime personas inside system-spec-kit command prompts
reason: Conflicts with existing senior-engineer voice, hard gates, and role-contract agent prompts; use personas for evaluation instead.
